import json
import re

# Load the civic docs query result from storage
path = var_call_4fX4CVxqrzrg2HK6TiF57mL4
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    docs = json.load(f)

spring_months = ['March 2022', 'April 2022', 'May 2022', 'Spring 2022', '2022-Spring', '2022 March', '2022-March', '2022 April', '2022-April', '2022 May', '2022-May']
month_regex = re.compile(r"(Spring 2022|2022-Spring|March 2022|April 2022|May 2022|2022-March|2022-April|2022-May|2022 March|2022 April|2022 May)", re.IGNORECASE)

# Keywords to help identify project title lines
title_keywords = ['Project', 'Repairs', 'Improvements', 'Park', 'Playground', 'Study', 'Plan', 'Facility', 'Repair', 'Restoration', 'Master Plan', 'Skate Park', 'Walkway', 'Traffic Study', 'Water Treatment', 'Green Streets', 'Retaining Wall', 'Drainage', 'Median', 'Signals', 'Slope', 'Biofilter', 'Trash Screens', 'Shade Structure', 'Road', 'Bridge']

found_projects = []

for doc in docs:
    text = doc.get('text', '')
    lines = [ln.strip() for ln in text.splitlines()]
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if month_regex.search(line):
            # search backwards for a plausible project title within previous 12 lines
            title = None
            for j in range(max(0, i-12), i)[::-1]:
                l = lines[j]
                if not l:
                    continue
                low = l.lower()
                if low.startswith('page') or low.startswith('agenda') or low.startswith('item'):
                    continue
                if l.startswith('(') and 'cid' in l.lower():
                    continue
                if ':' in l and len(l) < 30 and l.endswith(':'):
                    # skip short labels like "Updates:" or "Project Schedule:"
                    continue
                # if line contains any title keyword, take it
                if any(kw.lower() in low for kw in title_keywords):
                    title = l
                    break
                # otherwise, consider lines that look like titles: title-case and not too long
                if 3 < len(l) < 120 and sum(1 for c in l if c.isupper()) > 0:
                    title = l
                    break
            if title:
                # normalize whitespace
                title = re.sub(r"\s+", ' ', title).strip()
                found_projects.append(title)

# deduplicate preserving order
unique_projects = []
seen = set()
for p in found_projects:
    pk = p.lower()
    if pk not in seen:
        seen.add(pk)
        unique_projects.append(p)

# Print result as JSON list
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(unique_projects))

import json, re
path = var_call_4fX4CVxqrzrg2HK6TiF57mL4
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    docs = json.load(f)

# Patterns for starting in Spring 2022
patterns = [
    r"begin construction:\s*(spring|march|april|may)\s*2022",
    r"begin(ning)? in (the )?(spring|march|april|may) of 2022",
    r"will begin in (spring|march|april|may) 2022",
    r"begin(ning)? in (spring|march|april|may) 2022",
    r"begin construction:\s*(march|april|may) 2022",
    r"construction: (beginning )?(spring|march|april|may) 2022",
    r"begin construction: april 2022",
    r"begin construction: march 2022",
    r"begin construction: may 2022",
    r"begin in april 2022",
    r"begin in march 2022",
    r"begin in may 2022",
    r"beginning in spring 2022",
]
start_regex = re.compile("(" + ")|(".join(patterns) + ")", re.IGNORECASE)

# Keywords to identify project title lines
keywords = ['project','repairs','improvements','park','playground','study','plan','facility','repair','restoration','master plan','skate park','walkway','traffic study','water treatment','green streets','retaining wall','drainage','median','signals','synchronization','resurfacing','road','slope','trash screens','biofilter','water quality']

found = []
for doc in docs:
    text = doc.get('text','')
    lines = [ln.rstrip() for ln in text.splitlines()]
    for i,line in enumerate(lines):
        if start_regex.search(line):
            # search backward up to 40 lines for a plausible project title
            title = None
            for j in range(i-1, max(-1, i-60), -1):
                l = lines[j].strip()
                if not l:
                    continue
                low = l.lower()
                if low.startswith('page') or low.startswith('agenda') or low.startswith('item'):
                    continue
                if any(x in low for x in ['updates:', 'project schedule', 'project description', 'project updates', 'estimated schedule', 'project is', 'updates:','(cid:']):
                    continue
                # if line ends with 'Project' or contains 'Project'
                if 'project' in low or any(kw in low for kw in keywords):
                    # Clean up bullets and leading symbols
                    candidate = re.sub(r"^[\u2022\-\*\d\)\.]\s*", '', l)
                    candidate = re.sub(r"\s+", ' ', candidate).strip()
                    # avoid lines that are too long
                    if 3 < len(candidate) <= 200:
                        title = candidate
                        break
                # fallback title-case heuristic
                words = l.split()
                if 1 < len(words) <= 12 and sum(1 for w in words if w and w[0].isupper()) >= 1:
                    candidate = re.sub(r"\s+", ' ', l).strip()
                    title = candidate
                    break
            if title:
                found.append(title)

# Normalize names: remove leading cid markers, parentheses with agency tags kept
clean = []
seen = set()
for p in found:
    # remove leading markers like (cid:131)
    q = re.sub(r"^\(cid:\d+\)\s*", '', p)
    q = q.strip()
    # remove stray punctuation
    q = q.strip(' -:')
    key = q.lower()
    if key not in seen:
        seen.add(key)
        clean.append(q)

# Filter to probable project names: keep those with words like Road, Park, Project, Repairs, Improvements, Signal, Median, Walkway, Repair
keep_keywords = ['road','park','project','repairs','improvements','signal','median','walkway','slope','retaining','drain','water','skate','playground','traffic','bluffs','beach','green streets','canyon']
final = []
seen2 = set()
for p in clean:
    low = p.lower()
    if any(k in low for k in keep_keywords):
        if low not in seen2:
            seen2.add(low)
            final.append(p)

# Output unique final list
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(final))

                if any(x in low for x in ['updates:', 'project schedule', 'project description', 'project updates', 'estimated schedule', 'project is', 'updates:','(cid:']):
                    continue
                # if line ends with 'Project' or contains 'Project'
                if 'project' in low or any(kw in low for kw in keywords):
                    # Clean up bullets and leading symbols
                    candidate = re.sub(r"^[\u2022\-\*\d\)\.]\s*", '', l)
                    candidate = re.sub(r"\s+", ' ', candidate).strip()
                    # avoid lines that are too long
                    if 3 < len(candidate) <= 200:
                        title = candidate
                        break
                # fallback title-case heuristic
                words = l.split()
                if 1 < len(words) <= 12 and sum(1 for w in words if w and w[0].isupper()) >= 1:
                    candidate = re.sub(r"\s+", ' ', l).strip()
                    title = candidate
                    break
            if title:
                found.append(title)

# Normalize names: remove leading cid markers, parentheses with agency tags kept
clean = []
seen = set()
for p in found:
    # remove leading markers like (cid:131)
    q = re.sub(r"^\(cid:\d+\)\s*", '', p)
    q = q.strip()
    # remove stray punctuation
    q = q.strip(' -:')
    key = q.lower()
    if key not in seen:
        seen.add(key)
        clean.append(q)

# Filter to probable project names: keep those with words like Road, Park, Project, Repairs, Improvements, Signal, Median, Walkway, Repair
keep_keywords = ['road','park','project','repairs','improvements','signal','median','walkway','slope','retaining','drain','water','skate','playground','traffic','bluffs','beach','green streets','canyon']
final = []
seen2 = set()
for p in clean:
    low = p.lower()
    if any(k in low for k in keep_keywords):
        if low not in seen2:
            seen2.add(low)
            final.append(p)

# Output unique final list
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(final))
